name: Giants Causeway
description: The Giant's Causeway, located on the coast of Northern Ireland, is a remarkable natural wonder steeped in Irish mythology and folklore. Composed of approximately 40,000 interlocking basalt columns, the Causeway is a UNESCO World Heritage site and a focal point for tales of giants and heroic deeds. According to legend, the Causeway was built by the giant Fionn mac Cumhaill (Finn McCool) as a path to reach Scotland to confront his rival, the giant Benandonner. Fionn, after realizing Benandonner was much larger and stronger than him, was tricked by his wife, Oonagh, who disguised him as a baby. When Benandonner saw the 'baby' he assumed the father must be gigantic and fled back to Scotland, destroying the Causeway behind him. The remaining columns are thus seen as the remnants of this ancient pathway, a testament to the power and ingenuity attributed to the giants of Irish lore. The geological explanation attributes the columns to volcanic activity, but the mythological narrative provides a compelling and enduring explanation for its formation, solidifying its place as a sacred and mythical location in Irish culture. It continues to inspire awe and wonder, connecting the natural world with the vibrant stories of ancient Ireland. The landscape itself is a powerful reminder of the mythic past, and the Causeway serves as a place where the boundary between the real and the legendary blurs, inviting visitors to imagine the world of giants and heroes. The sheer scale and unique structure of the Causeway make it a natural canvas for the imagination, allowing the stories of Fionn mac Cumhaill to resonate with each new generation.
subtitle: Pathway of Giants, Irish Legend

keyMyths.title: Fionn and Benandonner
keyMyths.description: Fionn mac Cumhaill built the Causeway to fight Benandonner. However, he was tricked by his wife and Benandonner fled in fear, destroying part of the Causeway.

keyMyths.title: Geological Creation
keyMyths.description: While mythology attributes it to giants, scientists believe the Causeway was formed by volcanic activity millions of years ago. This scientific explanation coexists with the mythical narrative.

sources.author: Lady Wilde
